data.title: Home page hero with Sierpinski carpet photo over skyline

data.body: Replace current 3D Three.js fractal animation with Sierpinski carpet photo prototype. Keep NYC skyline background, place Sierpinski carpet in center. Source prototype: /Users/jules/Documents/dev/pretext/pages/demos/sierpinski-carpet-photo-responsive.html. Canvas-based, progressively reveals photo through fractal holes with text filling non-hole cells.

Review pass — APPROVED.

Reviewed against plan and acceptance criteria:

1. No Three.js at runtime: Hero.tsx no longer imports Suspense/lazy or FractalCityScene. Three.js remains in package.json (intentional — plan says to keep src/components/three/ for separate cleanup) but Vite tree-shakes it since nothing imports it from the active render path. Acceptance criterion met.

2. Skyline background: skyline4.png is present and rendered via absolute-positioned img with opacity 0.35 and existing clipPath/maskImage treatment. Preserved from prior implementation. ✓

3. Sierpinski carpet overlay: SierpinskiCarpet.tsx implements a recursive CSS grid pattern (3x3 grid, center cell = transparent hole, 8 recursive children). depth=3 produces ~658 DOM divs — within normal browser performance bounds, not a concern. hero-bg.png exists in public/images/. Image sits behind the carpet as a background, visible through holes. ✓

4. Hero text: 'An open source protocol for creating golden ages — starting in Brooklyn.' — matches PRD copy exactly. ✓

5. FadeIn preserved: FadeIn component wraps the hero text paragraph with delay={0.5}. ✓

6. Mobile responsive: Carpet uses 60vmin sizing (scales with viewport). Text uses responsive breakpoints (text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg, pb-12 md:pb-16). ✓

7. TypeScript: tsc --noEmit passed with zero errors. ✓

8. Two files changed, clean and minimal diff. No regressions in imports or component structure.

Minor note: 'Explore' CTA now links to #directory instead of #story — this is a deliberate update per the new PRD direction, not a bug.

No issues requiring rework. Acceptance criteria fully met.
